back_translation: Then Job answered:
'I have heard many things like these; miserable comforters are you all.
Will there be no end to your windy words? What provokes you to keep arguing?
I too could speak as you do, if you were in my place; I could string together words against you and shake my head at you in mockery.
But I would encourage you with my mouth, and the comfort of my lips would bring relief.
Even if I speak, my pain is not relieved; and if I keep silent, how will it go away?
Surely now You have worn me out; You have devastated my whole family.
You have bound me fast, and it has become a witness; my gauntness rises up and testifies against me to my face.
His anger tears me, and He hates me; He gnashes His teeth at me; my adversary fixes His piercing eyes on me.
People open their mouths at me; they strike my cheeks in contempt and band together against me.
God has handed me over to the unrighteous and flung me into the clutches of the wicked.
I was at ease, but He crushed me; He seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces; He has set me up as His target.
His archers surround me; He pierces my kidneys without mercy and pours out my gall on the ground.
He breaks through me with breach upon breach; He rushes at me like a warrior.
I have sewn sackcloth over my skin and buried my dignity in the dust.
My face is flushed red from weeping, and deep shadows ring my eyes,
though my hands are free of violence and my prayer is pure.
O earth, do not cover my blood, and let my cry for help find no resting place.
Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and my advocate is on high.
My friends scorn me as my eyes pour out tears to God.
Oh, that a man might plead with God as one pleads on behalf of his neighbor!
For when only a few years have passed, I will go the way from which there is no return.
